key_insight: Automobile oxy-acetylene welding waste can be upcycled into high-purity calcium carbonate nanoparticles with mixed calcite–vaterite crystallinity and strong mesoporous surface properties.
engineering_relevance: This turns hazardous welding waste into a functional nanomaterial candidate for coatings, corrosion-related interfaces, and electrochemically active or dielectric applications.
method: Recovered Ca(OH)2-rich welding waste as precursor
Synthesized and characterized CaCO3 nanoparticles using XRF, SEM–EDX, XRD, FTIR, XPS, TGA/DTA, BET, DFT, and EIS
Evaluated morphology, phase purity, thermal stability, porosity, and impedance behavior
evidence_signal: The abstract reports dominant calcium content, ~81% calcite with <19% vaterite, ~16.28 nm crystallite size, 134 m²/g BET surface area, and two EIS relaxation processes in 0.5 M H2SO4.
watch_item: The material contains minor impurities and a mixed-phase structure, so performance may vary with synthesis control and downstream coating or device integration.
paper_title: Synthesis, characterisation and electrochemical behaviour of calcium carbonate nanoparticles derived from automobile oxy-acetylene gas welding waste
